headlines.title: Oil Rallies Toward $120 As Middle East Supply Risks Spiral
headlines.summary: Oil prices extended their rally on Wednesday after reports emerged that the U.S. will tighten its blockade on Iranian ports, prolonging fears of reduced oil supply from the region, and as the UAE hit markets with its OPEC-exit announcement. Brent crude for June delivery jumped 6.

headlines.title: Hormuz Blockage Boosts Panama Canal Oil Tanker Traffic
headlines.summary: Oil tanker traffic through the Panama Canal has jumped in recent weeks as Asia scrambles to buy U.S. crude amid choked supply from the Middle East. The Panama Canal, the fastest route for American energy exports to Asia on smaller vessels, is at its busiest for U.S. crude exports

headlines.title: Venezuelan Crude Will Eventually Lower US Gas Prices: Chevron Exec
headlines.summary: The current average price for gasoline in the US is $4.17 per gallon, according to AAA’s April 28 data. That’s 15 cents higher than a week ago and $1.02 more than the year-ago average, or a 32% increase. President Trump recently said he expects higher gas prices to last until at

headlines.title: Another Russian Oil Facility Struck As Zelensky Touts Drone Capabilities
headlines.summary: A major fire reportedly broke out on Wednesday at a Russian oil pumping station in the Perm region in the Ural Mountains in a suspected drone attack just a day after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy touted the increasing range and accuracy of Ukrainian drones. headlines.title: Pakistan PM: Oil Import Costs Up 167% Since Iran War Began
headlines.summary: Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced on Wednesday that the country’s oil import bill has jumped 167% to $800 million per week amid the war in Iran from around $300 million weekly before the conflict began. Brent crude for June delivery was trading at $114.75 per bar

headlines.title: Phillips 66 Beats Q1 Estimates by $0.88 Per Share as Refining Margins Surge
headlines.summary: In an earnings season highlighted by a Middle East conflict that has sent oil prices soaring, Texas oil refiner Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) has reported first quarter adjusted earnings of $0.49 per share, easily beating Wall Street's consensus of a loss of $0.39 while net income came

headlines.title: EIA: US Crude Oil Inventories Crashing, But Holding Above Average
headlines.summary: Crude oil inventories in the United States decreased by 6.2 million barrels during the week ending April 24, according to new data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) released on Wednesday. The decrease brings commercial stockpiles to 459.5 million barrels accor

headlines.title: Barclays Sees UAE Oil Supply Growth Accelerating Post-OPEC
headlines.summary: After quitting OPEC and OPEC+, the United Arab Emirates (UAE) is set to grow its oil production faster when the current Hormuz crisis is over, according to analysts at Barclays. In a surprise announcement on Tuesday, the UAE said it is quitting OPEC and the wider OPEC+ alliance e

headlines.title: EU Warns Energy Crisis From Iran War Could Last Years
headlines.summary: The new energy crisis triggered by the Middle East conflict could hurt European consumers and industries for years, European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen said on Wednesday. "Our shared goal is now to see a lasting end to the war," von der Leyen told the European Parl

headlines.title: OPEC Will Survive UAE Exit, But Medium-Term Supply Threat Is Real
headlines.summary: The OPEC and OPEC+ groups are not expected to falter following the abrupt, but not necessarily surprising, exit of one of the biggest producers, the United Arab Emirates (UAE), according to consultancy Energy Aspects.
